'''Deniz Başkent''' is a Turkish-born Dutch auditory scientist who works on [[auditory perception]]. As of 2018, she is Professor of [[Audiology]] at the [[University Medical Center Groningen]], Netherlands.

== Biography ==

Born and raised in Turkey, Başkent trained as an electrical engineer, obtaining undergraduate and MS degrees in electrical engineering (emphasis on robotics) at [[Bilkent University]] in Ankara. Başkent then moved to Los Angeles, CA, where she obtained a PhD in [[Biomedical engineering]] from the [[University of Southern California]] in 2003 for her work on "[[Speech recognition]] under conditions of frequency-place compression and expansion",<ref>{{cite web |title=Speech recognition under conditions of frequency-place compression and expansion :: University of Southern California Dissertations and Theses |url=http://digitallibrary.usc.edu/cdm/ref/collection/p15799coll16/id/628617 |website=USC Digital Library |access-date=1 June 2018}}</ref> under supervision of [[Robert V. Shannon]] at the [[House Ear Institute]] in Los Angeles, CA. She then obtained a [http://nohrfoundation.org/ NOHR] Grant to fund a postdoc position in the same lab until the end of 2004.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Başkent |first1=Deniz |title=Speech recognition in normal hearing and sensorineural hearing loss as a function of the number of spectral channels |journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America |date=November 2006 |volume=120 |issue=5 |pages=2908–2925 |doi=10.1121/1.2354017 |pmid=17139748|bibcode=2006ASAJ..120.2908B }}</ref> Moving from Los Angeles to the Bay Area, Başkent took a position as Research Scientist at the [[Starkey Hearing Technologies|Starkey]] Research Center in Berkeley.<ref>{{cite web |title=Curriculum Vitae of prof. dr. D. (Deniz) Baskent {{!}} How to find us {{!}} About us {{!}} University of Groningen |url=https://www.rug.nl/staff/d.baskent/cv?lang=en |website=University of Groningen |access-date=1 June 2018 |language=en}}</ref>

In 2009, Başkent moved to the Netherlands after obtaining a [https://www.rug.nl/about-us/work-with-us/rff/rosalind-franklin/ Rosalind Franklin Fellowship] at the Department of [[Otorhinolaryngology]] of the [[University Medical Center Groningen]]. There, she founded the dB SPL (Deniz Başkent Speech Perception Lab) research group. Başkent received a Vidi grant from the [[Netherlands Organisation for Scientific Research]] in 2010,<ref>{{cite web |title=Prof.dr. D. (Deniz) Baskent  |url=https://www.narcis.nl/person/RecordID/PRS1323378/Language/en |website=Data Archiving and Network Services, [[NARCIS (National Academic Research and Collaboration Information System)|NARCIS]] |access-date=2 June 2018 |language=nl}}</ref> and a Vici grant from the same organisation in 2017.<ref>{{cite web |title=Millions worth of grants for four Groningen top researchers {{!}} News articles {{!}} News and Events {{!}} About us {{!}} University of Groningen |url=https://www.rug.nl/news/2017/02/millions-worth-of-grants-for-four-groningen-top-researchers |website=University of Groningen |date=17 February 2017 |access-date=2 June 2018 |language=en}}</ref> She was promoted full professor in 2014.

Başkent became a Fellow of the [[Acoustical Society of America]] in 2017.<ref>{{cite web |title=Fellows of the Society - Acoustical Society of America |url=https://acousticalsociety.org/fellows-of-the-society/#FELLOWS_2017 |website=Acoustical Society of America |access-date=1 June 2018}}</ref>

== Research ==

Başkent's principal research area focuses on [[speech perception]] with [[cochlear implant]]s and hearing impairment. Her approach is largely multidisciplinary, combining [[psychoacoustics]], [[psycholinguistics]] and [[biomedical engineering]]. Between 2010 and 2016, she has published numerous studies on [[Phonemic restoration effect|phonemic restoration]] and interrupted speech in listeners with hearing impairment.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Başkent |first1=Deniz |last2=Eiler |first2=Cheryl L. |last3=Edwards |first3=Brent |title=Phonemic restoration by hearing-impaired listeners with mild to moderate sensorineural hearing loss |journal=Hearing Research |date=February 2010 |volume=260 |issue=1–2 |pages=54–62 |doi=10.1016/j.heares.2009.11.007 |pmid=19922784|s2cid=124652 }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Başkent |first1=Deniz |last2=Chatterjee |first2=Monita |title=Recognition of temporally interrupted and spectrally degraded sentences with additional unprocessed low-frequency speech |journal=Hearing Research |date=December 2010 |volume=270 |issue=1–2 |pages=127–133 |doi=10.1016/j.heares.2010.08.011|pmc=2997937 |pmid=20817081}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Başkent |first1=Deniz |title=Effect of Speech Degradation on Top-Down Repair: Phonemic Restoration with Simulations of Cochlear Implants and Combined Electric–Acoustic Stimulation |journal=Journal of the Association for Research in Otolaryngology |date=9 May 2012 |volume=13 |issue=5 |pages=683–692 |doi=10.1007/s10162-012-0334-3|pmc=3441953 |pmid=22569838}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Saija |first1=Jefta D. |last2=Akyürek |first2=Elkan G. |last3=Andringa |first3=Tjeerd C. |last4=Başkent |first4=Deniz |title=Perceptual Restoration of Degraded Speech Is Preserved with Advancing Age |journal=Journal of the Association for Research in Otolaryngology |date=7 November 2013 |volume=15 |issue=1 |pages=139–148 |doi=10.1007/s10162-013-0422-z|pmc=3901857 |pmid=24198087}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Bhargava |first1=Pranesh |last2=Gaudrain |first2=Etienne |last3=Başkent |first3=Deniz |title=Top–down restoration of speech in cochlear-implant users |journal=Hearing Research |date=March 2014 |volume=309 |pages=113–123 |doi=10.1016/j.heares.2013.12.003 |pmid=24368138|s2cid=46211075 |url=https://hal.archives-ouvertes.fr/hal-02144526/file/PRCI%202013%2012%2019%20-%20Author.pdf }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Benard |first1=Michel Ruben |last2=Susanne Mensink |first2=Jorien |last3=Başkent |first3=Deniz |title=Individual differences in top-down restoration of interrupted speech: Links to linguistic and cognitive abilities |journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America |date=February 2014 |volume=135 |issue=2 |pages=EL88–EL94 |doi=10.1121/1.4862879|pmid=25234920 |bibcode=2014ASAJ..135L..88B |url=https://pure.rug.nl/ws/files/30944441/1.4862879.pdf }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Clarke |first1=Jeanne |last2=Gaudrain |first2=Etienne |last3=Chatterjee |first3=Monita |last4=Başkent |first4=Deniz |title=T'ain't the way you say it, it's what you say – Perceptual continuity of voice and top–down restoration of speech |journal=Hearing Research |date=September 2014 |volume=315 |pages=80–87 |doi=10.1016/j.heares.2014.07.002|pmid=25019356 |s2cid=2057345 |url=https://hal.archives-ouvertes.fr/hal-02144535/file/2014-06-25-Clarke-et-al_2013_HearRes-author.pdf }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Bhargava |first1=Pranesh |last2=Gaudrain |first2=Etienne |last3=Başkent |first3=Deniz |title=The Intelligibility of Interrupted Speech: Cochlear Implant Users and Normal Hearing Listeners |journal=Journal of the Association for Research in Otolaryngology |date=18 April 2016 |volume=17 |issue=5 |pages=475–491 |doi=10.1007/s10162-016-0565-9|pmc=5023536 |pmid=27090115}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Clarke |first1=Jeanne |last2=Başkent |first2=Deniz |last3=Gaudrain |first3=Etienne |title=Pitch and spectral resolution: A systematic comparison of bottom-up cues for top-down repair of degraded speech |journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America |date=January 2016 |volume=139 |issue=1 |pages=395–405 |doi=10.1121/1.4939962|pmid=26827034 |bibcode=2016ASAJ..139..395C }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Clarke |first1=Jeanne |last2=Kazanoğlu |first2=Deniz |last3=Başkent |first3=Deniz |last4=Gaudrain |first4=Etienne |title=Effect of F0 contours on top-down repair of interrupted speech |journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America |date=July 2017 |volume=142 |issue=1 |pages=EL7–EL12 |doi=10.1121/1.4990398|pmid=28764445 |bibcode=2017ASAJ..142L...7C |doi-access=free |hdl=11370/b2fb8fc7-5f1f-4bf4-9a8b-0b5adbb44e7d |hdl-access=free }}</ref>

More recently, her work has focused on voice, emotion and speaking style perception in cochlear implant users,<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Fuller |first1=Christina D. |last2=Gaudrain |first2=Etienne |last3=Clarke |first3=Jeanne N. |last4=Galvin |first4=John J. |last5=Fu |first5=Qian-Jie |last6=Free |first6=Rolien H. |last7=Başkent |first7=Deniz |title=Gender Categorization Is Abnormal in Cochlear Implant Users |journal=Journal of the Association for Research in Otolaryngology |date=30 August 2014 |volume=15 |issue=6 |pages=1037–1048 |doi=10.1007/s10162-014-0483-7|pmc=4389960 |pmid=25172111}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Gaudrain |first1=Etienne |last2=Başkent |first2=Deniz |title=Factors limiting vocal-tract length discrimination in cochlear implant simulations |journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America |date=March 2015 |volume=137 |issue=3 |pages=1298–1308 |doi=10.1121/1.4908235|pmid=25786943 |bibcode=2015ASAJ..137.1298G |url=https://hal.archives-ouvertes.fr/hal-02144542/file/Gaudrain_Bas%CC%A7kent_2015_JASA.pdf }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Gaudrain |first1=Etienne |last2=Başkent |first2=Deniz |title=Discrimination of Voice Pitch and Vocal-Tract Length in Cochlear Implant Users |journal=Ear and Hearing |date=2018 |volume=39 |issue=2 |pages=226–237 |doi=10.1097/AUD.0000000000000480|pmc=5839701 |pmid=28799983}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Başkent |first1=Deniz |last2=Luckmann |first2=Annika |last3=Ceha |first3=Jessy |last4=Gaudrain |first4=Etienne |last5=Tamati |first5=Terrin N. |title=The discrimination of voice cues in simulations of bimodal electro-acoustic cochlear-implant hearing |journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America |date=April 2018 |volume=143 |issue=4 |pages=EL292–EL297 |doi=10.1121/1.5034171|pmid=29716273 |bibcode=2018ASAJ..143L.292B |doi-access=free |hdl=11370/06d69058-a321-4525-a68f-15dd7f23c873 |hdl-access=free }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal |last1=Tamati |first1=Terrin N. |last2=Janse |first2=Esther |last3=Başkent |first3=Deniz |title=Perceptual Discrimination of Speaking Style Under Cochlear Implant Simulation |journal=Ear and Hearing |date=May 2018 |pages=63–76 |doi=10.1097/AUD.0000000000000591 |volume=40|issue=1 |pmid=29742545 |pmc=6319584 }}</ref><ref>{{Cite journal|title=Does Good Perception of Vocal Characteristics Relate to Better Speech-On-Speech Intelligibility for Cochlear Implant Users?|last1=N|first1=El Boghdady|last2=E|first2=Gaudrain|date=2019|language=en|pmid=30710943|last3=D|first3=Başkent|journal=The Journal of the Acoustical Society of America|volume=145|issue=1|page=417|doi=10.1121/1.5087693|bibcode=2019ASAJ..145..417E|doi-access=free|hdl=11370/aeaedf5b-3b07-4744-bcd5-ed9c2ea677ca|hdl-access=free}}</ref> development of voice and emotion perception in children with normal and impaired hearing,<ref>{{Cite journal|last1=Nagels|first1=Leanne|last2=Gaudrain|first2=Etienne|last3=Vickers|first3=Deborah|last4=Lopes|first4=Marta Matos|last5=Hendriks|first5=Petra|last6=Başkent|first6=Deniz|date=2020-04-02|title=Development of vocal emotion recognition in school-age children: The EmoHI test for hearing-impaired populations|journal=PeerJ|language=en|volume=8|article-number=e8773|doi=10.7717/peerj.8773|pmid=32274264|pmc=7130108|issn=2167-8359|doi-access=free}}</ref><ref>{{Cite perception is dissociated across gender cues in school-age children|journal=Scientific Reports|language=en|volume=10|issue=1|page=5074|doi=10.1038/s41598-020-61732-6|pmid=32193411|pmc=7081243|bibcode=2020NatSR..10.5074N |issn=2045-2322|doi-access=free}}</ref> cognitive and neural mechanisms of normal and impaired hearing,<ref>{{Cite journal|title=Neural Entrainment to Speech Modulates Speech
